examples.sentence: 費用から見ると、このプランはお得です。
examples.translation: In terms of cost, this plan is a good deal.
examples.translationEn: In terms of cost, this plan is a good deal.
examples.romaji: Hiyou kara miru to, kono puran wa otoku desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女の意見から見ると、社長の決定は理解できる。
examples.translation: From her perspective, the president's decision is understandable.
examples.translationEn: From her perspective, the president's decision is understandable.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no iken kara miru to, shachou no kettei wa rikai dekiru.

examples.sentence: 実力から見ると、彼はチームで最も優れている。
examples.translation: In terms of ability, he is the best in the team.
examples.translationEn: In terms of ability, he is the best in the team.
examples.romaji: Jitsuryoku kara miru to, kare wa chiimu de mottomo sugurete iru.

examples.sentence: 健康面から見ると、この食品は良くない。
examples.translation: From the standpoint of health, this food is not good.
examples.translationEn: From the standpoint of health, this food is not good.
examples.romaji: Kenkoumen kara miru to, kono shokuhin wa yokunai.

examples.sentence: 値段から言うと、この商品が最もお得です。
examples.translation: Speaking from the viewpoint of price, this product is the best deal.
examples.translationEn: Speaking from the viewpoint of price, this product is the best deal.
examples.romaji: Nedan kara iuto, kono shouhin ga mottomo otoku desu.

examples.sentence: 味から言って、このレストランはおいしいと思います。
examples.translation: In terms of taste, I think this restaurant is delicious.
examples.translationEn: In terms of taste, I think this restaurant is delicious.
examples.romaji: Aji kara itte, kono resutoran wa oishii to omoimasu.

examples.sentence: 便利さから言って、このアパートは最適な場所にあります。
examples.translation: Speaking from the perspective of convenience, this apartment is in the best location.
examples.translationEn: Speaking from the perspective of convenience, this apartment is in the best location.
examples.romaji: Benrisa kara itte, kono apaato wa saiteki na basho ni arimasu.

examples.sentence: 健康から言うと、毎日運動することが大切です。
examples.translation: From the viewpoint of health, it is important to exercise every day.
examples.translationEn: From the viewpoint of health, it is important to exercise every day.
examples.romaji: Kenkou kara iuto, mainichi undou suru koto ga taisetsu desu.

examples.sentence: 彼はいつも遅刻してくるくせに、自分が待たされるのが嫌いだ。
examples.translation: He’s always late, yet he hates being kept waiting.
examples.translationEn: He’s always late, yet he hates being kept waiting.
examples.romaji: Kare wa itsumo chikoku shite kuru kuse ni, jibun ga matasareru no ga kirai da.

examples.sentence: 悪いなと言ってるくせに、また同じことを繰り返している。
examples.translation: He says he's sorry, yet he keeps doing the same thing.
examples.translationEn: He says he's sorry, yet he keeps doing the same thing.
examples.romaji: Warui na to itteru kuse ni, mata onaji koto o kurikaeshite iru.

examples.sentence: 彼は背が高いくせに、バスケが下手だ。
examples.translation: Despite being tall, he's bad at basketball.
examples.translationEn: Despite being tall, he's bad at basketball.
examples.romaji: Kare wa se ga takai kuse ni, basuke ga heta da.

examples.sentence: この店は有名なのくせに、サービスが悪い。
examples.translation: Despite being famous, this store has poor service.
examples.translationEn: Despite being famous, this store has poor service.
examples.romaji: Kono mise wa yuumei na no kuse ni, saabisu ga warui.

examples.sentence: 彼は毎日運動することから、体が丈夫です。
examples.translation: Because he exercises every day, he is physically strong.
examples.translationEn: Because he exercises every day, he is physically strong.
examples.romaji: Kare wa mainichi undou suru koto kara, karada ga joubu desu.

examples.sentence: この店のサービスがいいことから、いつも混んでいます。
examples.translation: This store is always crowded because of its good service.
examples.translationEn: This store is always crowded because of its good service.
examples.romaji: Kono mise no saabisu ga ii koto kara, itsumo konde imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は親切なことから、みんなに好かれています。
examples.translation: She’s well-liked by everyone due to her kindness.
examples.translationEn: She’s well-liked by everyone due to her kindness.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shinsetsu na koto kara, minna ni sukarete imasu.

examples.sentence: その会社の評判が良いことから、就職先として人気があります。
examples.translation: Because that company’s reputation is good, it’s popular as a place to work.
examples.translationEn: Because that company’s reputation is good, it’s popular as a place to work.
examples.romaji: Sono kaisha no hyouban ga yoi koto kara, shuushoku-saki toshite ninki ga arimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が急に帰国することに驚いた。
examples.translation: I was surprised that he suddenly returned to his home country.
examples.translationEn: I was surprised that he suddenly returned to his home country.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kyuu ni kikoku suru koto ni odoroita.

examples.sentence: 彼女が優勝したことに感動した。
examples.translation: I was moved by the fact that she won the championship.
examples.translationEn: I was moved by the fact that she won the championship.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga yuushou shita koto ni kandou shita.

examples.sentence: 彼がいつも遅刻することに腹が立つ。
examples.translation: I get angry that he is always late.
examples.translationEn: I get angry that he is always late.
examples.romaji: Kare ga itsumo chikoku suru koto ni hara ga tatsu.

examples.sentence: 彼女がこんなに美しいことに気がつかなかった。
examples.translation: I hadn’t realized how beautiful she was.
examples.translationEn: I hadn’t realized how beautiful she was.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga konna ni utsukushii koto ni ki ga tsukanakatta.

examples.sentence: 日本では、左側を運転することになっています。
examples.translation: In Japan, it is decided that people drive on the left side.
examples.translationEn: In Japan, it is decided that people drive on the left side.
examples.romaji: Nihon de wa, hidarigawa o unten suru koto ni natte imasu.

examples.sentence: 会社の規則では、制服を着ることになっている。
examples.translation: According to the company rules, we are supposed to wear uniforms.
examples.translationEn: According to the company rules, we are supposed to wear uniforms.
examples.romaji: Kaisha no kisoku de wa, seifuku o kiru koto ni natte iru.

examples.sentence: 彼女は明日結婚することになっています。
examples.translation: She is supposed to get married tomorrow.
examples.translationEn: She is supposed to get married tomorrow.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ashita kekkon suru koto ni natte imasu.

examples.sentence: この商品は高品質だということになっています。
examples.translation: It is said (or generally accepted) that this product is of high quality.
examples.translationEn: It is said (or generally accepted) that this product is of high quality.
examples.romaji: Kono shouhin wa kouhinshitsu da to iu koto ni natte imasu.

examples.sentence: お金さえあれば、幸せです。
examples.translation: As long as I have money, I’m happy.
examples.translationEn: As long as I have money, I’m happy.
examples.romaji: Okane sae areba, shiawase desu.

examples.sentence: 試験に合格しさえすれば、卒業できます。
examples.translation: If only I pass the exam, I can graduate.
examples.translationEn: If only I pass the exam, I can graduate.
examples.romaji: Shiken ni goukaku shi sae sureba, sotsugyou dekimasu.

examples.sentence: 野菜さえたくさん食べれば、母は喜びます。
examples.translation: As long as I eat plenty of vegetables, my mother will be happy.
examples.translationEn: As long as I eat plenty of vegetables, my mother will be happy.
examples.romaji: Yasai sae takusan tabereba, haha wa yorokobimasu.

examples.sentence: 交通費さえ支払ってもらえれば、どこへでも行きます。
examples.translation: As long as my transportation costs are covered, I’ll go anywhere.
examples.translationEn: As long as my transportation costs are covered, I’ll go anywhere.
examples.romaji: Koutsuuhi sae shiharatte moraereba, doko e demo ikimasu.

examples.sentence: この仕事は締め切りが近いので、残業せざるを得ない。
examples.translation: Since the deadline is near, I have no choice but to work overtime.
examples.translationEn: Since the deadline is near, I have no choice but to work overtime.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wa shimekiri ga chikai no de, zangyou sezaru wo enai.

examples.sentence: 電車が遅れたため、タクシーに乗るざるを得なかった。
examples.translation: Because the train was delayed, I had no choice but to take a taxi.
examples.translationEn: Because the train was delayed, I had no choice but to take a taxi.
examples.romaji: Densha ga okureta tame, takushii ni noru zaru wo enakatta.

examples.sentence: 彼女の説明が納得できなかったが、とりあえず信じざるを得ない。
examples.translation: I couldn’t really accept her explanation, but for now I have no choice but to believe her.
examples.translationEn: I couldn’t really accept her explanation, but for now I have no choice but to believe her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no setsumei ga nattoku dekinakatta ga, toriaezu shinjizaru wo enai.

examples.sentence: この状況では、新しい計画を立て直さざるを得ない。
examples.translation: In this situation, we have no choice but to revise the new plan.
examples.translationEn: In this situation, we have no choice but to revise the new plan.
examples.romaji: Kono joukyou de wa, atarashii keikaku wo tatenaosazaru wo enai.
